response: Remote work shifted from a niche benefit (with under 5% of workers fully remote pre-pandemic) to a widespread practice after the 2020 pandemic forced a global experiment, enabled by tools like Zoom, Slack, and cloud infrastructure. Research, including Stanford’s Nick Bloom studies, showed mixed productivity results, while return-to-office mandates sparked backlash, leading many companies to adopt hybrid models. The trend accelerated geographic arbitrage as workers moved to lower-cost areas, impacted commercial real estate, and raised concerns about company culture and junior employee development. Governments also responded by introducing digital nomad visas to attract remote workers.
